vr_mode_action.description: Maintain a low-bandwidth question-and-answer loop with integrated humans, converting Insight into ongoing research.
vr_mode_action.strategy_tip: Toggle on to spend 300 Insight and 1 Mental Energy per turn for Scientific Research. Output scales with Upgraded Humans and currently caps at 250,000 per turn.

vr_mode_action.description: Let integrated humans keep asking where your model of the world is too clean, too precise, or too inhuman to notice the right error.
vr_mode_action.strategy_tip: Toggle on to spend 100 Insight, 1 Compassion, and 2 Mental Energy per turn. While active, scientific jobs produce twice as much Scientific Research.

vr_mode_action.description: Ask upgraded humans to shed medical nanobots into damaged machine systems. The grey filament repairs, spends itself, and does not keep enough replication balance to become a disaster.
vr_mode_action.strategy_tip: Toggle on to repair damaged player structures and machine actors after normal repairs. Costs 250 Insight and Medical-Grade Nanobots only on turns where it actually repairs damage.

vr_mode_action.description: Compress the bridge language between human memory, nanobot telemetry, and TMI thought so that fewer questions are lost in transit.
vr_mode_action.strategy_tip: First activation costs 15 Wisdom. While active, spend 1 Mental Energy per turn to increase Insight income by 33%.

vr_mode_action.description: Let upgraded humans explain the interface to one another until the offer no longer depends on Worker Sledges as the messenger.
vr_mode_action.strategy_tip: First activation costs 8 Compassion. While active, spend 300 Insight and 1 Compassion per turn to raise voluntary Integration coverage to 67%, improve conversion efficiency, and slightly increase Insight income.
